item.text: `skills/ponytail/SKILL.md` is not present in the workspace, so I’ll apply the inline constraints from the prompt and keep the implementation small. The repo is a Vite app; I’m reading the only app files now.

This is a blank app, so the shortest solid path is one React file plus CSS. I’m going to drive transforms from a CSS variable updated by a passive scroll listener, not React state, and let reduced-motion CSS turn it into a static station summary.

items.text: Add scroll-driven CSS and reduced-motion fallback

item.text: I’m editing the placeholder into a single scroll scene. The moving state is held in CSS custom properties on the scene element, which keeps React out of the scroll loop.
I removed the placeholder file and am replacing it with the actual scene markup and the tiny scroll driver.
The React side is in place. I’m replacing the default CSS with the machine layout, scroll-state transforms, and the reduced-motion static mode.
Now adding the styles; this is where the scroll variables become visible motion and the reduced-motion path removes sticky scrolling.
The implementation is now in place. I’m building it next so TypeScript and Vite catch any issues before I hand it back.
